LAW AND MOTION 301, ATTORNEY T. TROY OTUS' MOTION TO BE RELIEVED AS COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF BRIAN CARRILLO WAS HEARD TODAY. COUNSEL INDICATED THAT MAIL SENT TO CLIENT WAS RETURNED AS UNDELIVERABLE. THE COURT GRANTED THE MOTION SUBJECT TO COUNSEL SUBMITTING A DECLARATION WITH ATTACHED PROOF OF SERVICE RE RETURN RECEIPT REQUESTED AND AN INDICATION FROM THE POST OFFICE THAT MAIL IS UNDELIVERABLE. COUNSEL TO ALSO SUBMIT PROPOSED ORDER. JUDGE: JAMES L. WARREN, REPORTER: CHRISTINA PAXTON, CSR #1558

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Aug-29-2007 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES: No appearance. The court having reviewed the file finds no good cause or substantial justification for plaintiff's failure to comply with the court's orders and rules. Therefore, it is hereby ordered that this matter is dismissed. The court finds that a less severe sanction would not be effective due to the history of lack of compliance. Proceedings reported by: Patricia Van Steenberge CSR#1742 (212)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel-Civil
Off Calendar Apr-04-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: T. Troy Otus, Esq. of Law Firm Of Otus & Associates' Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel-Civil for PLAINTIFF BRIAN CARRILLO.
APPEARANCE: Stephen A. Roberts, Esq. of Law Firm Of Otus & Associates' 650/548-2100 for moving party; no other appearances.
RULING: Hearing held. Counsel indicated that mail sent to client was returned as undeliverable. The Court granted the motion subject to counsel submitting a declaration with attached proof of service re return receipt requested and an indication from the post office that mail is undeliverable. Counsel to also submit proposed order. (301/JLW/ju) Judge: James L. Warren, Reporter: Christina Paxton, CSR #1558
